en: Here the question arises whether it is still advisable to bring to light all the patient's phantasies by analysis, if we now consider them as of no ætiological significance. Psychoanalysis hitherto has proceeded to the unravelling of these phantasies because they were considered to be ætiologically significant. My altered view concerning the theory of neurosis does not change the procedure of psychoanalysis. The technique remains the same. We no longer imagine we are unearthing the end-root of the disease, but we have to pull up the sexual phantasies because the energy which the patient needs for his health, that is, for his adaptation, is attached to them. By means of psychoanalysis the connexion between the conscious and the libido in the unconscious is re-established. Thus you restore this unconscious libido to the command of conscious intention. Only in this way can the formerly split-off energy become again applicable to the accomplishment of the necessary tasks of life. Considered from this standpoint, psychoanalysis no longer appears to be a mere reduction of the individual to his primitive sexual wishes, but it becomes clear that, if rightly understood, it is a highly moral task of immense educational value .
zh: 此處便產生一個問題：既然我們如今認為病人的所有幻想並不具有病因學上的意義，那麼透過分析將這些幻想揭露出來，是否仍屬明智之舉？精神分析迄今為止之所以致力於釐清這些幻想，乃是因為它們被視為具有病因學上的重要性。我對神經症理論的觀點雖已改變，但這並未改變精神分析的實際操作程序。技術依然如故。我們不再以為自己是在挖掘疾病的根本根源，但我們仍須將性幻想挖掘出來，因為病人為恢復健康——亦即為適應環境——所需的能量，正依附於這些幻想之上。透過精神分析，意識與潛意識中的力比多之間的聯繫得以重新建立。如此一來，你便能將這股潛意識的力比多重新置於意識意圖的掌控之下。唯有透過這種方式，原先被分裂出去的能量，才能再次應用於完成生活中必要的任務。從這個角度來看，精神分析不再僅僅是將個體還原至其原始的性慾望，而是清楚地顯示：若正確理解，它實則是一項具有高度道德性、且蘊含巨大教育價值的任務。
